title: RUSTPY-0018 — PyAtomicRef<T> Debug is unsound (casts Py<T> as T); _asyncio._enter_task's {:?} error message reaches it and SIGSEGVs on any Python-function task (ext.rs:272 via _asyncio.rs:2492)
one_line_repro:
import _asyncio
def f(): pass
_asyncio._enter_task(0, f)
_asyncio._enter_task(0, f)  # SIGSEGV
notes: MINIMAL SIGSEGV REPRO DONE (5 lines, deterministic 5/5, gdb-confirmed identical to vehicle; a plain function suffices -- no hostile object). Two bugs: (1) DEEPER/ROOT -- PyAtomicRef<T>::Debug (ext.rs:272) is unsound: it .cast::<T>()s a pointer that actually points at Py<T> (every other method -- Deref :301, load_raw :324, From :288, swap :331 -- casts to Py<T>), skipping the object header and reading misaligned fields. PyFunction.code is PyAtomicRef<PyCode>, so {:?} on a function reaches CodeObject::Debug (bytecode.rs:1308) which derefs garbage obj_name/source_path heap pointers -> SIGSEGV. Fix = one char: .cast::<T>() -> .cast::<Py<T>>() (Py<T>: Debug at core.rs:2261). (2) TRIGGER -- _asyncio._enter_task (_asyncio.rs:2492) formats the tasks with {:?} (Debug) not Python repr in its 'Cannot enter' RuntimeError; wrong even without the crash (dumps the internal Rust struct). Fix = task.repr(vm)?. Builtin control (len, no PyCode) does NOT crash -> isolates the PyCode path. Other {:?}-of-pyobject sites that can independently hit bug 1: typevar.rs:933/:996, os.rs:946/:1069. Was 46/57 of fleet-08's mislabeled 'recursion SIGSEGV' bucket. Distinct from 0007a (linear Debug chain, not self-recursion). CPython uses %R (repr).
